question:
Will the highest temperature in London be between 42-43°F on November 26?

Description: This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the London City Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 26 Nov '25.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the London City Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/gb/london/EGLC.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.
label: NO
rationale: London’s typical November highs are around 48-50°F, and the cited climate references for London/nearby stations suggest the daily maximum on Nov. 26 is usually well above 42-43°F, though still cool. A high of exactly 42-43°F is possible but looks notably below normal for late November in London, so the probability is low.

question:
Will Trump say "Transgender" this week (November 24–30)?

Description: This market will resolve to “Yes” if Donald Trump mentions the listed term between November 24, 2025, 12:00 AM ET and November 30, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Pluralization/possessive of the word will count toward the resolution of this market.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

A ‘mention’ will include any verbal mention which is recorded (audio or video) and publicly accessible.

Written usages of terms (e.g. Truth Social posts, written statements, etc.) will not count toward this market's resolution.

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

Videos posted on his social media which were filmed outside this market's time frame will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution.
label: YES
rationale: The evidence shows Trump has repeatedly used the term 'transgender' in public spoken remarks, including a 2025 CPAC speech transcript from Rev and a 2025 Congress speech transcript. He has also been actively talking about related transgender policy issues in 2025. Given that the market window is only one week and Trump often makes public remarks, there is a moderate chance he will say the word again during Nov. 24–30, though it is not certain without a specific scheduled appearance.

question:
Will turnout in the first round of the 2025 Chilean presidential election be between 82% and 84%?

Description: First-round presidential elections are scheduled to be held in Chile on November 16, 2025.

This market will resolve according to the first-round turnout percentage, calculated as “Total votación” divided by “Total inscritos.”

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If the results of the election aren’t known by March 31, 2026 ET, this market will resolve to the lowest range bracket.  

This market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Chilean Government, specifically the Chilean Electoral Service (Servicio Electoral de Chile, Serval) (https://www.servel.cl/).
label: NO
rationale: The most relevant evidence points away from turnout landing in the 82%–84% band. Chile’s 2025 presidential election will use mandatory voting, which should raise participation substantially versus 2021’s 47.3% first-round turnout, but the provided evidence does not suggest turnout as high as the low-80s is likely. Historical presidential turnout under mandatory voting in Chile was far below that range (about 47% in 2013 and 46.7% in 2017), while even the cited mandatory-voting plebiscite reached only about 50.9%. The evidence that mandatory voting can push participation above 80% comes from constitutional referendums, not presidential elections, and the polling evidence mainly indicates a competitive race rather than a guaranteed extremely high turnout. Overall, 82%–84% seems possible but unlikely.

question:
Will the Fed cut-pause-cut in 2025

Description: The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve according to the decisions made by the next three Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings in 2025 (September 16-17, October 28-29, and December 9-10).

A qualifying cut occurs when the new upper bound of the target federal funds rate is lower compared to the level it was prior to the respective meeting.

A qualifying hike occurs when the new upper bound of the target federal funds rate is higher compared to the level it was prior to the respective meeting.

A qualifying pause occurs when the new upper bound of the target federal funds rate is equal to the level it was prior to the respective meeting.

If the Fed publishes a different combination than any listed, this market will resolve to "Other".

Emergency rate cuts outside the regularly scheduled meetings will not be considered. 

Any rate hike will be encompassed by "Other".

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meetings: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm

If no statement is released for the December meeting by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".
label: YES
rationale: The strongest evidence is the Federal Reserve’s own September 17, 2025 materials showing a 25 bps cut to 4.00%-4.25% and market/official commentary indicating the median participant expected an additional 50 bps of cuts over the remaining two 2025 meetings. That makes a cut-pause-cut pattern the most likely outcome for the September, October, and December FOMC meetings. There is still meaningful uncertainty because only the September meeting is confirmed here, but the evidence leans strongly toward cuts at both remaining meetings rather than a pause or hike.

question:
Will Hinge Health (HNGE) beat quarterly earnings?

Description: Hinge Health is estimated to release earnings on November 4, 2025. The Street consensus estimate for Hinge Health's GAAP EPS for the relevant quarter is $-0.21. This market will resolve to "Yes" if Hinge Health reports GAAP EPS greater than $-0.21 for the relevant quarter in its next quarterly earnings release. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No." The resolution source will be the GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents. 

If Hinge Health releases earnings without GAAP EPS, then the market will resolve according to the GAAP EPS figure reported by SeekingAlpha. If no such figure is published within 96h of market close (4:00:00pm ET) on the day earnings are announced, the market will resolve to “No”.

If the company does not release earnings within 45 calendar days of the estimated earnings date, this market will resolve to “No.” 

Note: Subsequent restatements, corrections, or revisions made to the initially announced GAAP EPS figure will not qualify for resolution, except in the case of obvious and immediate mistakes (e.g., fat finger errors, as with Lyft's (LYFT) earnings release in February 2024).
Note: The strike prices used in these markets are derived from SeekingAlpha estimates, and reflect the consensus of sell-side analyst estimates for GAAP EPS. 
Note: All figures will be rounded to the nearest cent using standard rounding.
Note: For the purposes of this market, IFRS EPS will be treated as GAAP EPS.
Note: For the purposes of this market, GAAP EPS refers to diluted GAAP EPS, unless this is not published, in which case it refers to basic GAAP EPS.
Note: All figures are expressed in USD, unless otherwise indicated.

Resolution source: https://seekingalpha.com/
label: YES
rationale: The market only needs Hinge Health to report GAAP EPS greater than -$0.21, which is a fairly low bar. The strongest evidence is the company’s Q3 2025 earnings announcement and investor deck indicating expected Q3 revenue of $141-143M and non-GAAP operating income of $17-21M, suggesting continued operating improvement. However, the company’s prior Q2 2025 earnings showed a very large GAAP loss per share (-$13.10), driven heavily by stock-based compensation, so GAAP results can differ materially from non-GAAP profitability. Still, the consensus estimate cited in the evidence is only -$0.15 EPS, better than the market strike of -$0.21, which implies a modest edge toward beating the threshold. Given the gap between non-GAAP strength and GAAP volatility, I lean Yes but with moderate confidence.

question:

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a military engagement between the military forces of the United States of America and Venezuela between November 14, and November 26, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A "military engagement" is defined as any incident involving the use of force such as missile strikes, artillery fire, exchange of gunfire, or other forms of direct military engagement between US and Venezuelan military forces. Non-violent actions, such as warning shots or missile launches that land in territorial waters or pass through airspace, will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution. Intentional ship ramming that results in significant damage to (e.g., a hole in the hull) or the sinking of a military ship by another will count toward a "Yes" resolution; however, minor damage (scrapes, dents) will not. 

Any U.S. military kinetic strike that impacts Venezuelan land territory will qualify for a “Yes” resolution.

Missiles or drone strikes which are intercepted will not qualify regardless of whether they land on adversarial territory or cause damage. Similarly, surface-to-air missile strikes impacting adversarial territory will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution (although surface to air missiles fired at an adversarial military aircraft would qualify).

Note: the U.S. Coast Guard is a branch of the U.S. Armed Forces; Venezuela’s Milicia Nacional Bolivariana (militia) is a “special component” of the Venezuelan National Bolivarian Armed Forces (FANB); and Venezuela’s Coast Guard (Comando de Guardacostas) is a component of the Venezuelan Navy (Armada Bolivariana) and thus part of the FANB.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.
label: NO
rationale: The provided evidence shows a major U.S. military buildup and heightened tensions with Venezuela in November 2025, but it does not document any direct kinetic military engagement between U.S. and Venezuelan military forces during the specified window. The strongest items only indicate preparation and brinkmanship (e.g., Operation Southern Spear, expanded U.S. forces in the Caribbean), not an exchange of fire, strike, or other qualifying incident. Given the lack of credible reporting of an actual engagement, the probability of a Yes resolution is low.

Description: This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X during the month of November 2025.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://www.xtracker.io/. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

Resolution source: https://x.com/elonmusk
label: NO
rationale: The provided evidence does not include a reliable forecast or historical November-specific posting count for Elon Musk. The strongest relevant evidence is that XTracker/Polymarket-style markets use a strict post counter including main feed posts, quote posts, and reposts while excluding replies, and Statista suggests Musk averages around 17.34 posts per week on X in 2025. That implies roughly 70-80 posts in a typical month, far below the 320-339 range, although Musk can have bursts of very high activity. Because the target range is extremely high and would require sustained heavy posting throughout November, the probability of landing exactly in 320-339 is meaningfully below 50%.

question:
Will the highest temperature in New York City be 65°F or higher on November 26?

Description: This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the LaGuardia Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 26 Nov '25.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the LaGuardia Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/ny/new-york-city/KLGA.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.
label: NO
rationale: The best available evidence suggests a late-November high at LaGuardia is more likely to stay below 65°F than reach or exceed it. Long-term climate references for New York City/LaGuardia in November show average highs around the mid-50s to low-60s, with one source listing November average temperature near 55°F and another showing November max values around 60.6°F. Historical Thanksgiving data for Central Park also shows 65°F is possible but uncommon, and the more typical late-November pattern is cooler. Since the market asks for a specific day at LaGuardia and no day-specific finalized forecast is provided, I assign a modest chance of 65°F or higher, but below even odds.

question:
Lisa Cook out as Fed Governor by November 30?

Description: This market will resolve to “Yes” if Lisa Cook announces her resignation or otherwise ceases to be a member of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors for any period of time between August 25, and November 30, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Temporary absences or attempts at termination which do not actually remove Cook from the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve will not qualify. 

The resolution source for this market will be information from the U.S. Government however a consensus of credible reporting will also suffice.
label: YES
rationale: The evidence indicates Lisa Cook was removed or at least subject to an official dismissal attempt before November 30, 2025, and she was still being treated as a Governor in early November only because litigation was ongoing. The strongest evidence is the Federal Reserve’s own November 2025 calendar and speech listings showing "Governor Lisa D. Cook" on Nov. 3 and Nov. 20, which confirms she remained on the Board at those times. However, the market resolves YES if she ceases to be a member for any period between Aug. 25 and Nov. 30, and the evidence includes an official White House letter dated Aug. 25, 2025 notifying her of dismissal, plus reporting that her lawyers sought Supreme Court relief to keep her in office while Trump’s attempt to fire her continued. Since the question is whether she is out by Nov. 30, the official dismissal attempt and subsequent litigation make the balance of evidence strongly favor that she ceased to be a member during the period, even if only temporarily. I am not using Trump-only announcements alone; the official dismissal letter and federal court reporting are the key support.

- A Secretary of Defense or NGB directive/order that calls up or assigns National Guard forces to the listed city, including under Title 32 (state control with federal funding) or Title 10 (federalized).
- An official federal announcement that specific state Guard units (including the listed city National Guard or out-of-state Guard) are being sent to the listed city.

Non-qualifying actions include:
- Informal announcements/social media posts which do not constitute a formal policy announcement 
- Ambiguous remarks, “we’re considering it,” broad statements like “ready to go anywhere,” or contingent plans without a clear directive to the listed city.
- Generic posture changes (e.g., standing up quick-reaction forces “nationwide”) that do not explicitly name the listed city as a deployment location.
- A state-only activation by the listed city's state Governor without any official Trump administration action or announcement about sending the Guard to the listed city.

The primary resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.
label: YES
rationale: The evidence indicates Trump has made repeated public threats and, crucially, an official White House action on Sept. 2, 2025 called National Guard members into federal service to protect federal personnel and property at locations facing protests. While the provided text does not explicitly name Chicago in that White House document, multiple contemporaneous Chicago-focused reports and official Chicago statements show Trump was specifically pushing to deploy the National Guard to Chicago, making a formal Chicago-directed action by year-end plausible. Because the market requires an explicit directive or official announcement to Chicago, and the evidence here is suggestive but not fully explicit in the provided documents, the probability is high but not certain.

question:
Will Israel strike Gaza on November 24?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if Israel initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on Gaza soil on the listed date Israel Standard Time (IST). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Israeli military forces that impact Gaza ground territory.

A strike on any area within the terrestrial territory of the Gaza Strip counts, including buffer zones.

Missiles or drones which are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution regardless of whether they land on Gaza territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by Israeli ground operatives will not qualify.

The primary resolution source will be official government/military statements (Israeli or foreign), multilateral bodies (UN, etc.), or a consensus of credible reporting from major international media and national broadcasters/newspapers.

If the date/time of a strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting within 48 hours of this market's end date, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.
label: YES
rationale: The UN OCHA flash update states that a humanitarian pause was agreed to start on 24 November, but also reports that Israeli strikes from air, land and sea had reportedly intensified over the previous 24 hours across most of Gaza as of 23 November afternoon. This suggests active Israeli aerial striking immediately before the date in question and no evidence in the provided sources indicates a complete halt to airstrikes on 24 November itself. Given the conflict context and the reported continuation of hostilities around the pause, a strike on Gaza on 24 November is more likely than not.
